title: Yamazakia viscosa
taxonomy: Yamazakia viscosa is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is native to tropical Africa and is also known as sticky bamboo or sticky bush. The classification of Yamazakia viscosa places it within the order Gentianales. The plant's sticky nature is due to the presence of viscid secretions on its stems and leaves. Yamazakia viscosa is often found growing in humid, tropical environments.

intro: Yamazakia viscosa, also known as sticky yam, is a plant species native to Southeast Asia, belonging to the family Lamiaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, contributing to its potential health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and boosting the immune system.
